title: What Mike Vrabel said about A.J. Brown’s skillset, cost, and rocky exit from Philly
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Mike Vrabel confirms Patriots traded a first-round pick in 2028 and a fifth-round pick in 2027 for A.J. Brown.
Vrabel praised A.J. Brown's versatility and skill set, noting his strong performance with 8,029 yards and 56 touchdowns since 2019.
A.J. Brown's departure from the Eagles was contentious, but Vrabel is focused on integrating Brown into the Patriots' system.

title: Gov. Healey backs bill to keep Mass. bars open until 3 a.m. this summer
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Gov. Maura Healey supports a bill to extend Massachusetts bar hours to 3 a.m. from June 1 to Aug. 31, 2026.
The bill, sponsored by Rep. Carole Fiola, coincides with events like the World Cup and the nation's 250th anniversary.
Boston Mayor Michelle Wu also supports the proposal, currently pending before the Joint Committee on Economic Development.

title: A.J. Brown makes his Patriots practice debut and other takeaways from Day 2 of OTAs
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: A.J. Brown made his practice debut with the Patriots, participating lightly and catching a one-handed pass in drills.
Quarterback Maye produced a turnover-free performance, connecting on several short passes with a few standout deep throws.
The Patriots placed tight-end Julian Hill on injured reserve, with Coach Vrabel confirming he will miss the entire season.

title: Market Basket’s ex-CEO, Arthur T. Demoulas, says he won’t fight court ruling upholding his firing
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Arthur T. Demoulas won't appeal the ruling upholding his removal as Market Basket CEO, citing company interests.
Delaware Court confirmed the board's authority to oust Demoulas after tensions and allegations of planned work stoppage.
Demoulas's sisters, controlling 61% of Market Basket, are accused of orchestrating his removal to seize family control.

title: MBTA and Boston announce plans for Summer Street closures during World Cup
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Summer Street in Boston will close to vehicles for eight hours on select World Cup match days.
Street closure is between Dorchester and Atlantic avenues; plans include temporary security setups at South Station.
MBTA withdrew its eminent domain letter; Boston will grant occupancy permits to manage event safety.

title: Man allegedly involved in wrong-way Route 1 crash detained by ICE before arraignment
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Lucas Gustavo Brajak DeAlmeida Di Benedetto, accused in a Route 1 crash, was detained by ICE before his arraignment.
The Peabody crash involved a State Police trooper's cruiser, with minor injuries reported, and occurred early Sunday morning.
Di Benedetto was released on $500 bail, facing charges of DUI and negligent vehicle operation after the wrong-way crash.

title: All the new movies and TV shows streaming in June
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Season 3 of 'House of the Dragon' premieres June 21 on HBO Max.
Disney+ streams 'Hoppers' on June 3 and 'Avatar: Fire and Ash' on June 24.
Hulu showcases summer blockbusters including 'Independence Day' and 'Talladega Nights'.

title: Police looking to ID man after stabbing outside South Boston dive bar
author: John Waller
bullets: Police seek to identify a man involved in a South Boston stabbing outside Croke Park bar early Sunday morning.
The victim, found with multiple stab wounds, was hospitalized but injuries are not life-threatening, police report.
Suspects fled on foot toward C Street; police urge anyone with information to contact them at 617-343-4742.

title: Yankee Candle founder’s estate sells for far below asking price, will become ‘luxury getaway’
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Yankee Candle founder's estate in Leverett sold for $4.55 million, vastly below its $23 million asking price.
The estate features a water park, golf course, and bowling alley, reflecting its resort-like amenities.
PGA golfer David Reasoner and his wife plan to transform the property into a luxury getaway and event space.

title: Patriots’ Will Campbell recently got engaged
author: John Waller
bullets: Patriots offensive tackle Will Campbell is engaged to Ashlynn Nussmeier, who shared the news on Instagram.
Ashlynn Nussmeier, daughter of Saints' OC Doug Nussmeier, attends LSU where Campbell played before being drafted.
Campbell's fiancée credits her brother, Garrett Nussmeier, with introducing her to Will Campbell at LSU.
